hooks.command: file="$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH"; if echo "$file" | grep -qE '\.(ts|tsx)$' && echo "$file" | grep -qE '^(apps/|packages/)'; then echo 'TypeScript file modified — run the cleanup skill and typecheck before committing'; fi
file="$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH"; if echo "$file" | grep -q 'packages/db/prisma/generated'; then echo 'BLOCKED: Do not write to generated Prisma files' && exit 1; fi

hooks.prompt:
A `git push` is about to run. Automatically security-review the outgoing changes before it proceeds.

1. Run `git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD` (if it errors, `git diff --name-only HEAD~10..HEAD`) to list changed files.
2. For each security-relevant file — any apps/api/src/** (controller, service, guard, auth, trigger job, integration), Next.js route handler (app/api/**, route.ts), Prisma query, upload/webhook handler, or any file containing raw SQL ($queryRaw/$executeRaw), dangerouslySetInnerHTML, child_process/exec/spawn/eval, a signed-URL/S3 put, or a by-id .findUnique/.findFirst/.update/.delete — review it against the checklist in .claude/agents/security-reviewer.md: broken access control, tenant isolation & IDOR, injection, XSS, secrets, SSRF, auth/session, unsafe uploads, mass assignment. Read the code and trace guard->controller->service->DB; verify, do not speculate.
3. If you find any unaddressed P1 or P2 issue, BLOCK the push and list each as: severity, file:line, the concrete exploit, and the fix. If there are no security-relevant changes or nothing high-severity, ALLOW the push.

Be precise: only block on a confirmed high-severity issue, never on style or speculation.
